title: ## **Balance Sheet Overview – As of March 31, 2026**
content:
DATA CHECK: The figure for 'Other Assets' (863) is implausibly small relative to Total Assets (19,067), suggesting a potential unit or extraction error in the source data; this item will be omitted from the narrative.

## **Balance Sheet Overview – As of March 31, 2026**
The most significant balance-sheet development for Northern Valley during this period was the contraction across its asset portfolio. Total assets stood at $19,067 million as of March 31, 2026, decreased by 4.7%.

Cash and deposits declined to $4,895 million, down 7.1%, resulting in a smaller near-term liquidity position. Loans and leases fell to $12,109 million, down 4.3%. Other investments remained largely flat at $1,200 million.

Loans and leases is now the largest component of the balance sheet. That contraction on the asset side was funded primarily through shares/deposits, reflecting how the institution's holdings were paid for during the period.

Total liabilities stood at $15,818 million, decreased by 6.8%, led by shares/deposits at $15,739 million, down 6.9%. Accounts payable and accrued interest on borrowings increased to $50 million, up 24.4%.

Total equity stood at $2,649 million, decreased by 8.8%, supported by undivided earnings of $2,604 million and stable reserves. Taken together, the decline in loans was matched largely by a reduction in member funding sources. Management continues to watch the pace of loan contraction relative to deposit withdrawal.
